channel.title: UK Think Tank Jobs
channel.description: Job listings for Think Tanks roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Speculative Application
item.description: The European Council on Foreign Relations (ECFR) is an award-winning international think-tank that aims to conduct cutting-edge independent research on European foreign and security policy and to provide a safe meeting space for decision-makers, activists and influencers to share ideas. We build coalitions for change at the European level and promote informed debate about Europe’s role in the world. In 2007, ECFR’s founders set about creating a pan-European institution that could combine establishment credibility with intellectual insurgency. Today, ECFR remains uniquely placed to continue providing a pan-European perspective on some of the biggest strategic challenges and choices Europeans need to confront, with a network of offices in seven European capitals, over 90 staff from more than 25 different countries and a team of associated researchers in the EU 27 member states. For more information check our website: ecfr.eu
item.dc:creator: European Council on Foreign Relations

item.title: Programme Coordinator - Asia (m/f/d)
item.description: The Programme Coordinator plays a key role in ensuring the smooth operation and strategic coordination of the programme’s activities. Working closely with the Programme Director and Deputy Director, they ensure effective collaboration with internal teams, national offices, policy fellows, and external stakeholders. The role involves cross-functional coordination in areas such as advocacy, financial management, fundraising, and communications. The coordinator ensures effective project delivery and may contribute to other programme initiatives, enhancing the overall impact of ECFR’s work and the programme’s objective. Support the implementation of the Programme’s strategy and work plan under the guidance of the Deputy Director. Coordinate the development and execution of advocacy and communications strategies, ensuring alignment with ECFR’s national offices. Organize and manage all aspects of Programme events, including private meetings, workshops, consultations, and public events. Oversee the deliverables and Programme’s financial management , including budget drafting, monitoring, reporting, and ensuring compliance with grant/donor regulations. Coordinate fundraising efforts and contribute to the preparation of relevant materials and approaches for prospective donors in collaboration with the Strategic Partnerships team. Help strengthen and maintain relationships with current donors, and support donor reporting and grant management. Build and maintain a strong advocacy network by liaising with external contacts, partners, ECFR council members, and relevant stakeholders. Develop and maintain ECFR’s global contact database to support outreach and Programme communications. Track and report on the Programme’s impact to ECFR Leadership Team, Board of Trustees, and external donors/stakeholders. Support effective integration of the programme’s interns, acting as main point of contact and coordinating onboarding, providing guidance, and ensuring they have the resources need
item.dc:creator: European Council on Foreign Relations

item.title: PPCA Secretariat Coordinator, London
item.description: PPCA Secretariat Coordinator, Powering Past Coal Alliance (PPCA), London This London-based role will support delivery of the PPCA Secretariat by coordinating events, steering committee and member engagement, and internal processes ensuring smooth day-to-day operations across the PPCA. Background The Powering Past Coal Alliance (PPCA) is a coalition of over 180 members working to advance the transition from unabated coal power generation to clean energy. Founded in 2017 by the United Kingdom and Canada its membership now includes almost a third of the world’s countries, financial institutions, sub-national governments in the most coal-dependent regions in the world and leading utilities. The PPCA Secretariat is hosted by E3G, an independent climate change think tank, which provides management and administrative support to the Secretariat, plus strategic analysis and advice. E3G is the world lead strategist think tank on the political economy of climate change with a global outlook. We work on the frontier of the climate landscape tackling the barriers and advancing the solutions to a safe climate for all. We translate climate politics, economics and policies into action. We pride ourselves on bringing independence to an extremely polarised discussion and holding policy makers to account on their promises. E3G is consistently ranked as one of the top-ten most influential environmental think tanks in the world by the University of Pennsylvania’s Global Go To Think Tank Index. E3G is dedicated to fostering an engaging work environment with a competitive remuneration package in the non-profit sector. E3G offers a dynamic and fast paced working environment that will value your insight and initiative wherever you are in the organisation. We take pride in our team's commitment, supportiveness, and friendliness. We invest in our staff, offer flexible working conditions and pride ourselves on being an open and collaborative workplace. Role Overview The PPCA Secretariat Coordinator plays a central role in supporting the effective delivery of the Powering Past Coal Alliance (PPCA) Secretariat’s work. The role coordinates events, member engagement, governance meetings and operational processes, ensuring smooth collaboration across a global network of governments, businesses and civil society partners. Working closely with the Secretariat Manager, Head of Secretariat and wider E3G teams, the Coordinator provides high-quality logistical, administrative and stakeholder support that enables the PPCA to advance its international mission and deliver impact across a fast-paced and dynamic policy environment. Key Responsibilities Lead preparation for key events Coordinate logistical preparation and delivery of PPCA events, including agendas, timelines, invitations and supporting materials. Manage event logistics such as venue liaison, travel arrangements and scheduling. Maintain and update the PPCA contact database, calendar and forward plan of key meetings and milestones. Conduct background research to support preparation of meetings, briefings and deliverables. Organise meetings and delegations with senior stakeholders Support coordination of meetings and delegations involving senior political, civil society and media stakeholders. Manage logistics including scheduling, travel coordination, agendas and briefing materials. Support the Head of Secretariat with meeting scheduling. Membership coordination Coordinate communication and engagement with PPCA members. Organise PPCA governance and member meetings, including PPCA Steering Committee, Implementation Committee and Members Meetings. Maintain accurate records of member contacts, engagement and correspondence. Work with the communications team and Secretariat Manager to coordinate progress updates on PPCA work. Operations and Internal Coordination Support the development of effective ways of working and communication for a growing Secretariat team based across multiple time zones. Support recruitment processes, including coordinating applications, interviews and candidate correspondence. Strengthen cross-team coordination by contributing to the development and maintenance of internal tools and processes that support events and Secretariat activities. Support drafting of internal documents, reports and correspondence as required. Provide updates on PPCA activities to E3G internal communications, ensuring PPCA updates are shared at staff forums and newsletters. Provide flexible administrative and coordination support to the Secretariat Manager as required. About You Motivated by E3G’s mission and keen to gain hands-on experience in policy-oriented research and stakeholder engagement in an international setting. Several years’ relevant professional experience and track record in a relevant setting. High level of initiative and be positive, organised, resilient, and able to deliver high-quality work on time. Skills & Experience Experience of events management. Experience of building relationships and networks, and working with partner organisations, coalitions, and networks including senior stakeholders. Experience of project and budget management. Prince 2 or other project management training or qualification would be considered relevant but not essential. Very strong written communication skills and advanced written and spoken English. What We Offer Permanent, full-time position. Work-life balance. Enjoy a 35-hour workweek with a hybrid work model and flexitime. 25 vacation days + additional leave between Christmas and New Year. Pension contribution plan E3G offers a reward and benefits package which compares well with other organisations within our sector. The indicative salary range in the UK for this position is between £41,498 - £44,000, dependent on experience and qualifications of the chosen candidate. Yearly process for remuneration review. E3G will require candidates to demonstrate that they have the right to live and work in the jurisdiction in which E3G employs them. Occasional international travel may be required from time to time. We are unable to provide any relocation assistance to candidates who would need to move in order to take up this position. Your Application Please email your application in English and include: A CV of maximum two pages A cover letter of maximum one page outlining why you are applying for this role and when you would be able to start. Recruitment Process First online interview with HR and Team Members Final interview with HR, Secretariat Manager, Head of the PPCA Secretariat The deadline for applications is July 31, 2026. Whilst we very much appreciate the time you will have taken with your application, unfortunately due to the level of response we receive we will not be able to reply to every candidate. Therefore, if you have not heard from us within 2 weeks of the closing date, please assume that on this occasion you have been unsuccessful. E3G values diversity and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified candidates regardless of age, gender, race, disability, sexual orientation, religion or ethnic background. We aim to build an inclusive workforce that is reflective of the populations we support, and actively seek applications from those who are marginalized and underrepresented. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: E3G

item.title: Programme Support Intern - Events & Advocacy
item.description: The purpose of the internship is to provide support for event organization, administration, and social media communication related to EU foreign policy and international relations, while gaining practical experience in a dynamic, multinational environment. Event and Advocacy Support : Assist with the organization and logistics of events and outreach activities for the Sofia office, including tracking RSVPs, booking accommodations and transportation, and managing event-related communications. Social Media and CRM Management : Contribute to managing the program’s social media presence, assist with maintaining the CRM system, and support the network engagement and communication efforts. Translation and Administrative Support : Translate documents from English to Bulgarian. Provide general office support and assist with day-to-day administrative tasks. Team Collaboration : Work closely with the Sofia coordination team as well as Communications and Advocacy departments and provide flexible support for a variety of tasks . Current university student (Bachelor’s or Master’s ideally in their last year ) in Political Science, International Relations, Economics, Media Studies , Social Sciences, or a related discipline. University Agreement is required . Strong academic background in political science with a focus on international relations, EU foreign policy, or economics. Proficiency in written and spoken English and Bulgarian . Organizational Skills : Well-organized, with the ability to multi-task effectively, prioritize responsibilities, and maintain attention to detail. Proactivity and Flexibility : Proactive, self-motivated, and flexible, able to work both independently and as part of a multinational team. Communication and Interpersonal Skills : Strong communication skills, with ease in drafting invitations, engaging in social interactions, and presenting ideas. Comfortable working in a multicultural environment and able to adapt communication styles accordingly. Technical Proficiency : Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint), experienced in using tools like C anva , CRM systems , and WordPress , and proficient in social media platforms to help increase engagement Reliability and Quality Focus : Detail-oriented, reliable, and committed to delivering high-quality work with a positive attitude. Teamwork and Management : Capable of managing tasks efficiently, prioritizing and multitasking in a fast-paced environment, with strong interpersonal and social skills that contribute to team success. Flexible and hybrid working mode An exciting environment in an innovative, pan-European think-tank An open and appreciative culture within the organi s ation High level of personal responsibility . Committed m ulticultural and diverse colleagues from across Europe A modern workplace in the heart of Sofia . If this is of interest, we look forward to receiving your application! Please use the apply button to submit your cover letter and CV in English (max. 2 pg. and pdf format) by the 5th of July 2026, 18:00. Candidates must be eligible to work in Bulgaria Please note that this internship is unpaid and for those who are willing to have a learning experience for three months, ideally from 15th July to 15th October (we are flexible in terms of dates) We aim to keep you posted with the status of your application, but due to high volume of applicants, we are unable to communicate with all applicants individually. If you are not contacted within 2 weeks of closing date, please consider you have not been pre-selected. We care about your data privacy. If you are interested to know more about how we manage your personal data, you can find more information in this link: https://ecfr.eu/privacy-job-applicants/ ECFR is an Equal Opportunity Employer and we do not discriminate against applicants due to race, ethnicity, gender, veteran status, or on the basis of disability or any other federal, state or local protected class. STRICTLY NO AGENCIES PLEASE
item.dc:creator: European Council on Foreign Relations

item.title: Senior Communications Officer
item.description: Senior Communications Officer, PPCA, Singapore This post based in Singapore, to lead the PPCA’s communications work to enhance the public debate on the benefits and feasibility of the coal-to-clean transition in Southeast Asia and contribute to PPCA’s wider comms activities, reporting to the Communications Lead. Who We Are The Powering Past Coal Alliance (PPCA) is a coalition of over 180 members working to advance the transition from unabated coal power generation to clean energy. Founded in 2017 by the United Kingdom and Canada its membership now includes almost a third of the world’s countries, financial institutions, sub-national governments in the most coal-dependent regions in the world and leading utilities. The PPCA Secretariat is hosted by E3G, an independent climate change think tank, which provides management and administrative support to the Secretariat, plus strategic analysis and advice. E3G is the world lead strategist think tank on the political economy of climate change with a global outlook. We work on the frontier of the climate landscape tackling the barriers and advancing the solutions to a safe climate for all. We translate climate politics, economics and policies into action. We pride ourselves on bringing independence to an extremely polarised discussion and holding policy makers to account on their promises. E3G is consistently ranked as one of the top-ten most influential environmental think tanks in the world by the University of Pennsylvania’s Global Go To Think Tank Index. E3G is dedicated to fostering an engaging work environment with a competitive remuneration package in the non-profit sector. E3G offers a dynamic and fast paced working environment that will value your insight and initiative wherever you are in the organisation. We take pride in our team's commitment, supportiveness, and friendliness. We invest in our staff, offer flexible working conditions and pride ourselves on being an open and collaborative workplace. Role Overview Lead the PPCA’s communications work to enhance the public debate on the benefits and feasibility of the coal-to-clean transition in Southeast Asia and contribute to PPCA’s wider comms activities, reporting to the Communications Lead. Key Responsibilities Communication Strategy : Work with the PPCA Comms Team, the broader PPCA team, and with relevant governments (PPCA co-chairs: the UK and Canada; CTC co-chairs: France and Indonesia) to lead, develop and deliver comprehensive communication strategies that effectively communicate PPCA’s value proposition to our key target audience, closely following the political and media debates in Southeast Asia to identify where strategic communications can be usefully deployed in order to speed up the coal-to-clean transition. Messaging : Develop and refine compelling and impactful narratives that effectively address local priorities, concerns, and opportunities while creating a compelling vision of a prosperous, resilient future powered by clean energy. Identify regional proof-points and case studies, as well as leverage closed-door PPCA engagements to generate exclusive insights. Operationalise narratives with a wide range of communications material (e.g., press releases, press briefings, social media content, op-eds, blogs, communication support during events), ensuring consistency across all communication channels. Media Relations : Bring a strong understanding of the media landscape in Southeast Asia to the PPCA Team. Cultivate strategic media partnerships through consistent engagement, positioning PPCA and its spokespeople as a valued resource for regional energy transition insights. Proactively identify opportunities to engage the media and pitch stories, deliver media assets, develop speaking points and other supportive material for interviews. Stakeholder Engagement and Events : Establish new partnerships and leverage existing ones to refine and amplify messages and identify and elevate local influential voices in comms activities. Use social media to increase visibility and create and strengthen connections. Coordinate online or in-person events for the PPCA Team, co-creating the initial concept and seeing them through to completion. Support the PPCA team in representing the organisation at other relevant events to establish relationships and enhance the Alliance’s profile. Monitoring and Evaluation : Track and analyse the impact of PPCA’s communications efforts in Southeast Asia, including media coverage, social media tracking and stakeholder feedback. Provide regular reports to senior staff and funders on the effectiveness of communication strategies and identify areas for improvement. Contribute to the broader comms activities of the PPCA’s comms team as required. About You A strong track record of building and maintaining impactful media relations in Southeast Asia, including pitching stories and securing media coverage. An enthusiasm to embrace complex, technical topics, and transform them into compelling narratives about the benefits and feasibility of the energy transition. The versatile and adaptable skills necessary to flourish in a fast-paced and wide-ranging work environment and work across time zones. Capability to meet deadlines independently in fast-paced environments. Fluency in written and spoken English, our working language; additional languages are an asset. Skills & Experience At least five years of professional experience in leading external communications, including developing and implementing communication strategies. Proven experience in working in networks with a strong track record of leading on stakeholder management. Demonstrated ability to work with a diverse range of stakeholders, including governments, think tanks and financial institutions. Proven experience in delivering events. Understanding of digital comms tools and platforms, including social media management, website content management systems, and email marketing. What We Offer Permanent, full-time position. Work-life balance. Enjoy a 35-hour workweek with a hybrid work model and flexitime. 25 vacation days + additional leave between Christmas and New Year. Pension contribution plan (can vary according to location). E3G offers a reward and benefits package which compares well with other organisations within our sector. The indicative salary range in the Singapore is $72,000 SGD - $76,800 SGD dependent on experience and qualifications of the chosen candidate. Yearly process for remuneration review. E3G will require candidates to demonstrate that they have the right to live and work in the jurisdiction in which E3G employs them. Occasional international travel may be required from time to time. We are unable to provide any relocation assistance to candidates who would need to move in order to take up this position. Your Application Are you ready to embark on an exhilarating journey where your ambition meets passion? Submit your application in English with a CV of maximum 2 pages and an offer letter of no more than 1 page outlining why you are applying for this role and when you would be able to start. Please combine into a one document submission. Recruitment Process First online interview with HR and Team Members Final online interview with HR, PPCA Communications Lead, and Head of Secretariat The deadline for applications is 31 July 2026. Whilst we very much appreciate the time you will have taken with your application, unfortunately due to the level of response we receive we will not be able to reply to every candidate. Therefore, if you have not heard from us within 2 weeks of the closing date, please assume that on this occasion you have been unsuccessful. E3G values diversity and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified candidates regardless of age, gender, race, disability, sexual orientation, religion or ethnic background. We aim to build an inclusive workforce that is reflective of the populations we support, and actively seek applications from those who are marginalized and underrepresented. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: E3G

item.title: Programme & Events Intern - Paris Office
item.description: The purpose of the internship is two-fold: 1) provide support for event organization, administration, and social media communication of the Paris office and 2) assist in a research project on the challenges of decarbonization, climate action and competitiveness in France, in the context of the 2027 presidential election. The internship will combine desk research on party programs, candidates, intellectual debates on these topics, and, when possible, in-person interviews and participation to conferences. The intern will gain practical experience in a dynamic, multinational environment. Event and Advocacy Support: Assist with the organization and logistics of events and outreach activities for the Paris office, including tracking RSVPs, booking accommodations and transportation, and managing event-related communications. Social Media and CRM Management: Contribute to managing the program’s social media presence, assist with maintaining the CRM system, and support the network engagement and communication efforts. Research Project Support: Assist with research on French debates on climate, decarbonization, and competitiveness in the context of the 2027 presidential election , draft research papers and prepare research briefings. Translation and Administrative Support: Provide general office support, including processing orders for supplies, handling logistics for staff events (e.g., retreats, parties), and assisting with day-to-day administrative tasks. Team Collaboration: Work closely with the Paris coordination team , the DARE team as well as Communications and Advocacy departments and provide flexible support for a variety of tasks, ensuring smooth operations and assisting with urgent needs as they arise. Current university student (Master’s) in Political Science, International Relations, Economics, Social Sciences, or a related discipline. A Convention de stage with your university is required . Strong academic background in political science with a focus on international relations, EU foreign policy, or economics Fluent in French and English Organizational Skills: Well-organized, with the ability to multi-task effectively, prioritize responsibilities, and maintain attention to detail. Proactivity and Flexibility: Proactive, self-motivated, and flexible, able to work both independently and as part of a multinational team. Communication and Interpersonal Skills: Strong communication skills, with ease in drafting invitations, engaging in social interactions, and presenting ideas. Comfortable working in a multicultural environment and able to adapt communication styles accordingly. Technical Proficiency: Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and experienced in using tools like CRM systems , Canva and WordPress. Reliability and Quality Focus: Detail-oriented, reliable, and committed to delivering high-quality work with a positive attitude. Teamwork and Management: Capable of managing tasks efficiently, prioritizing and multitasking in a fast-paced environment, with strong interpersonal and social skills that contribute to team success. Stipend according to local laws and regulations (around 4.50 euros per hour for 35 hours/week) Flexible and hybrid working mode Lunch Voucher Generous leave policy An exciting environment in an innovative, pan-European think-tank An open and appreciative culture within the organization High level of personal responsibility. Committed multicultural and diverse colleagues from across Europe A modern workplace in the heart of Paris Practical experience in a dynamic, multinational environment. If this is of interest, we look forward to receiving your application! Please use the apply button to submit your cover letter and CV (max. 2 pg. and pdf format) Application Deadline: 20th July 2026 Candidates must be eligible to work in France, enrolled as a student, and should be available from mid-September 2026 to mid-March 2027 We aim to keep you posted with the status of your application, but due to high volume of applicants, we are unable to communicate with all applicants individually. If you are not contacted within 2 weeks of closing date, please consider you have not been pre-selected. We care about your data privacy. If you are interested to know more about how we manage your personal data, you can find more information in this link: https://ecfr.eu/privacy-job-applicants/ ECFR is an Equal Opportunity Employer and we do not discriminate against applicants due to race, ethnicity, gender, veteran status, or on the basis of disability or any other federal, state or local protected class. STRICTLY NO AGENCIES PLEASE
item.dc:creator: European Council on Foreign Relations

item.title: Senior Policy Advisor, Trade Diplomacy, Japan
item.description: Senior Policy Advisor, Trade Diplomacy, Japan E3G is looking for a Senior Policy Advisor to lead analysis and engagement on trade diplomacy within its Japan programme. The new role will sit within E3G’s Global Clean Power Diplomacy team and will work closely with our Japan and trade experts to develop E3G’s work on the intersection of Japan’s trade policy, energy and economic security strategies, and climate objectives – with trade playing a key role as a diplomatic lever across these areas contributing to accelerating the clean energy transition. The role The role will build on existing work on trade and its use as a diplomatic lever, leading the development of strategies and engagements to align Japan’s trade, and economic policies, with the goals of a clean energy transition and ambitious climate action. The candidate will be based in Japan and will be required to work remotely. We are looking for an intellectually curious, enthusiastic person with experience of condensing and communicating complex information into clear, targeted analysis and advice for varied stakeholders. You will be motivated by building relationships with external stakeholders, and by creating impactful outcomes through strategic collaborations. You will have the opportunity to develop the strategy and shape the work programme, in consultation with colleagues in the Japan team and across E3G’s wider trade and industry related teams. Background E3G is an independent climate change think tank with a global outlook. We are world leading strategists on the political economy of climate change, dedicated to achieving a safe climate for all. We work on the frontier of the climate landscape tackling the barriers and advancing the solutions to a safe climate. Our goal is to translate climate politics, economics and policies into action. E3G is consistently ranked as one of the top ten most influential environmental think tanks in the world by the University of Pennsylvania’s Global Go To Think Tank Index. Delivering a rapid, managed transition of the global power system from coal-to-clean, not via gas, is a fundamental condition to achieving the goals of the Paris Agreement. E3G’s Global Clean Power Diplomacy team mobilises diplomatic actors to drive real-world, real-economy progress on ending the global use of the coal power and transitioning to clean energy. E3G identifies opportunities for influence and interventions, collaborates with civil society campaigns and partners, and fosters international diplomatic alliances to create a world where no new coal is built, and where existing coal is replaced by clean, affordable energy to enable a climate-safe world. Key Responsibilities Provide strategic insight and thought leadership on trade and diplomatic engagement with Japan in the context of broader global climate and energy goals. Monitor and track developments relating to Japan’s trade relationships with a focus on key partner countries, including various bilateral and multilateral frameworks and agreements (e.g. FTAs), government strategies and publications (e.g. Green Transformation Strategy; and industrial policy priorities, such as the 17 Strategic Fields under Japan’s growth strategy etc.), as well as commercial drivers relating to both traditional and clean energy supply chains, in order to assess their impact on Japan’s energy and economic security priorities and identify opportunities for strategic interventions. Produce analysis, presentations, briefings and other materials, both internally to contribute to E3Gs strategic thinking, and to a wide range of external contacts to communicate our analysis and recommendations, in both written and spoken formats. Expand E3G’s relationships and networks to relevant institutions and stakeholders in trade, energy and economic security – particularly civil society organisations (CSOs), alongside government officials, research institutions, trade associations and key private sector actors, primarily in Japan and key trade partner countries, to develop productive collaborations that support achieving the programme’s objectives. In collaboration with E3G’s Japan experts and trusted partners, maintain and widen the team’s relationships with diplomats and foreign missions and trade representatives to distribute analysis and strategic advice, for example through organising and presenting in online and in-person briefings. Represent E3G externally through active participation in civil society meetings, as well as relevant conferences and other events in Japan and internationally as needed; and maintain relations to media and provide commentary and insights about their area of expertise when needed. Experience and Skills A graduate degree in a relevant discipline, such as trade policy, international relations, climate and energy policy, development, geography, or politics. A minimum of six years of relevant professional experience in a related field, such as trade, policy, research or advocacy within a government or international organisation, commercial sector, think tank, civil society or research institute. Established connections to organisations and individuals relevant for the role, e.g. government and ministries, trade associations or related research institutions, and the ability to leverage these relationships effectively and in alignment with organisational priorities. Strong analytical and strategic thinking, with an aptitude for learning new areas and consider complex information to make evidence-based recommendations. Excellent communication skills, able to convey information and arguments to others in a clear, accessible and convincing way including in sensitive or challenging stakeholder contexts. Highly organised and attention to detail, able to be proactive and deliver high-quality outputs in a timely manner. Strong interpersonal skills, with ability to form high-trust relationships with various external stakeholders and handle sensitive information with discretion, as well as patience and resilience in long-term engagement processes. Excellent written and spoken Japanese and English. Ability to work effectively and collaboratively in an international team environment. What we offer Initial contract of one year, with possibility of extension, for a full-time position. Fully remote and flexible working arrangements. 16 vacation days + additional leave between Christmas and New Year. Eligible for social (employee health) insurance and employment insurance. E3G offers a competitive salary and reward package which compares well with other organisations within our sector, reflecting experience and qualifications of the chosen candidate. The salary shall be subject to withholdings for taxes and social and labor insurances in Japan mentioned above in which the Senior Policy Advisor will be enrolled. Yearly process for remuneration review. E3G is unable to provide relocation assistance to candidates who would need to change locations in order to take up this position. E3G will require candidates to demonstrate that they the right to live and work in Japan. How to apply Please submit your application in English. Please include: A CV of maximum two pages A cover letter of no more than one page outlining why you are applying for this role and when you would be able to start. Please include a written description of your residency status and right to work in Japan (for example, your visa status, if applicable), in the letter. The deadline for applications is Midnight, Sunday 16th August. Whilst we very much appreciate the time you will have taken with your application, unfortunately due to the level of response we receive we may not be able to reply to every candidate. Therefore, if you have not heard from us with 2 weeks of the closing date, please assume that on this occasion you have been unsuccessful. E3G values diversity and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified candidates regardless of age, gender, race, disability, sexual orientation, religion or ethnic background. We aim to build an inclusive workforce that is reflective of the populations we support, and actively seek applications from those who are marginalized and underrepresented. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: E3G

item.title: Senior IT Support Analyst
item.description: We don’t just talk, we do. Lead the change with us. At the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, we work with political leaders around the world to drive change. We help governments turn bold ideas into reality so they can deliver for their people. We do it by advising on strategy, policy and delivery, unlocking the power of technology across all three. And by sharing what we learn on the ground, so everyone can benefit. We do it to build more open, inclusive and prosperous countries for people everywhere. We are a global team of over 800 changemakers, operating in more than 40 countries, across five continents. We are political strategists, policy experts, delivery practitioners, technology specialists and more. We speak more than 45 languages. We are working on over 100 projects, tackling some of the world’s biggest challenges. We’re all here at TBI to make a difference. In a world of ever more complex challenges, we believe diversity of background and perspective is a strength. We pride ourselves on a culture that values and nurtures difference. We are dedicated to unlocking potential, not only for the countries we work in but also for each of our team members. No matter where you’re from or who you are, if you’re passionate about the transformative power of progressive politics, we invite you to build a better future with us. Role Summary Key Responsibilities Global User Support: Provide responsive 1st and 2nd line technical support to TBI’s end users, enabling them to undertake their roles in a secure and professional environment. Working collaboratively with our helpdesks in London, Nairobi and Dubai to handover tickets and tasks, ensuring that our staff receive timely assistance wherever they are located globally. Incident Management: Respond to and resolve IT service requests and incidents via phone, email, chat or in-person, ensuring timely resolution and maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction. Troubleshooting: Diagnose and troubleshoot hardware and software, escalating more complex issues to senior analysts. Onboarding: Onboarding new members of staff, ensuring that their IT services, laptop and other devices are ready to go on day one. Asset Management: Working with the ITAM team to ensure all end user IT assets are captured and the asset management system is kept up to date. Procurement: Working with Procurement to manage suppliers across the region, ensuring that we get the very best value and services for TBI. Knowledge Base: Contribute to the development and maintenance of a knowledge base, creating and updating support documentation and user guides. Security: Working with the Cyber Security team to assist in maintaining the security compliance across the end user environment (laptop, mobile devices) including multi factor authentication, anti-virus protection and threat detection. Compliance: Ensure all IT activities comply with company policies, procedures, and security standards. Business application support: Provide 1st line support for business applications. Person Specification Qualifications Diploma or Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Computer Engineering or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience. Experience 3–5 years' experience providing first- and second-line IT support within a corporate or enterprise environment. Experience supporting Windows and macOS devices in a Microsoft 365 environment. Experience supporting senior executives and VIP users. Experience working within a global or multi-site Service Desk environment is highly desirable. Essential Skills Proven experience working in an onsite IT Support, Service Desk or End User Computing role. Excellent customer service and communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical users. Strong analytical, troubleshooting and problem-solving skills. Self-motivated with the ability to take initiative, work independently and proactively resolve issues. Strong collaboration skills with the ability to work effectively as part of a global, distributed team. Excellent organisational skills with the ability to prioritise competing tasks and meet tight deadlines. Ability to lead technical tasks, projects or problem resolution when required. Willingness to work a flexible schedule on occasion to support global operations, including some evenings and weekends. Technical Skills Operating Systems Windows 10/11 macOS iOS Microsoft Technologies Microsoft 365 Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) Exchange Online Endpoint & Device Management Mobile Device Management (MDM) solutions Collaboration & Service Management Slack ITSM platforms or other Service Desk ticketing systems Desirable Skills Experience working in a head office or global corporate environment. Experience supporting a global Service Desk function. Knowledge of Salesforce. Experience using Freshservice or similar ITSM platforms. Experience administering or supporting JAMF. Experience supporting video conferencing technologies. Knowledge of cyber security best practices and endpoint security. Closing Date: 2026-07-30
item.dc:creator: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change

item.title: Talent Pool Contingent Upon Funding - Policy & Delivery Expert - Energy Access Programme
item.description: We don’t just talk, we do. Lead the change with us. At the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, we work with political leaders around the world to drive change. We help governments turn bold ideas into reality so they can deliver for their people. We do it by advising on strategy, policy and delivery, unlocking the power of technology across all three. And by sharing what we learn on the ground, so everyone can benefit. We do it to build more open, inclusive and prosperous countries for people everywhere. We are a global team of over 800 changemakers, operating in more than 40 countries, across five continents. We are political strategists, policy experts, delivery practitioners, technology specialists and more. We speak more than 45 languages. We are working on over 100 projects, tackling some of the world’s biggest challenges. We’re all here at TBI to make a difference. In a world of ever more complex challenges, we believe diversity of background and perspective is a strength. We pride ourselves on a culture that values and nurtures difference. We are dedicated to unlocking potential, not only for the countries we work in but also for each of our team members. No matter where you’re from or who you are, if you’re passionate about the transformative power of progressive politics, we invite you to build a better future with us. Role Summary Our client is seeking a Policy & Delivery Expert to support the design, coordination, and implementation of strategic energy sector initiatives. The role will focus on strengthening policy development, supporting programme delivery, and facilitating collaboration across government institutions, development partners, and private sector stakeholders. Role and Responsibilities Support development and implementation of strategic policy and programme initiatives. Contribute to programme planning, implementation frameworks, and delivery approaches. Support stakeholder engagement and coordination across multiple institutions. Produce technical analyses, recommendations, and briefing materials. Support programme monitoring, reporting, and performance management. Contribute to institutional strengthening and knowledge-sharing initiatives. Requirements Experience Required Minimum 8 years of experience in policy, advisory, reform, or delivery roles. Experience working with government institutions and development partners. Demonstrated policy drafting and implementation experience. Technical Requirements Advanced degree in Public Policy, Economics, Development Studies, Energy Policy, Law, or related field. Strong understanding of policy development and programme implementation. Experience supporting strategic planning and delivery processes Other Skills Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Effective stakeholder engagement capability. Working Arrangement Based full-time in Lilongwe, with periodic travel within Malawi as required. Terms of Agreement Start Date: To be confirmed Contracting Period: 12 months Important Notice This exercise is intended to identify and establish a pool of qualified candidates for this potential future opportunity. Participation does not constitute an offer of employment or guarantee appointment to any position. Closing Date: 2026-06-30
item.dc:creator: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change

item.title: Senior Manager – Health, Africa
item.description: We don’t just talk, we do. Lead the change with us. At the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, we work with political leaders around the world to drive change. We help governments turn bold ideas into reality so they can deliver for their people. We do it by advising on strategy, policy and delivery, unlocking the power of technology across all three. And by sharing what we learn on the ground, so everyone can benefit. We do it to build more open, inclusive and prosperous countries for people everywhere. We are a global team of over 800 changemakers, operating in more than 40 countries, across five continents. We are political strategists, policy experts, delivery practitioners, technology specialists and more. We speak more than 45 languages. We are working on over 100 projects, tackling some of the world’s biggest challenges. We’re all here at TBI to make a difference. In a world of ever more complex challenges, we believe diversity of background and perspective is a strength. We pride ourselves on a culture that values and nurtures difference. We are dedicated to unlocking potential, not only for the countries we work in but also for each of our team members. No matter where you’re from or who you are, if you’re passionate about the transformative power of progressive politics, we invite you to build a better future with us. Role Summary The Senior Manager – Health is a senior expert role within TBI Africa. The role will work closely with Africa leadership to shape and advance TBI’s health portfolio across the continent. You will collaborate with global experts across TBI, the fundraising team, and country and project teams to develop, refine, and align solutions for political leaders in the health sector. You will provide globally recognised, practical subject-matter expertise to TBI country teams and clients. This will support client engagements, programme and project design, key deliverables and fundraising activity, ensuring that we consistently bring the best of TBI to our health work in Africa. To succeed in this role, you will engage collaboratively across advisory, policy, and politics teams to deliver bold, evidence-based solutions, drawing on TBI’s growing body of thought leadership and strategic partnerships. This is a largely client-facing role and involves significant continental travel (approximately 30%). Key Responsibilities This is a senior role within TBI, suited to an exceptional candidate with deep experience and strong networks across in the health sector in Africa. Subject-Matter Expertise Act as a technical expert and thought partner to advisory teams across Africa, advancing a portfolio of work aligned with senior political leaders’ priorities in health reform and institutional capacity strengthening, and enabling better care for the public. Develop innovative, practical solutions to client challenges and present them in a compelling manner Engage, as needed, with senior stakeholders (e.g. Heads of State, Ministers, and other senior officials) to advance projects and solutions Support Advisory leadership in delivering high-quality advice, including ideation and problem-solving to ensure excellence in delivery and real-world impact. Contribute subject-matter expertise to support recruitment of health specialists across the organisation, including in-country embedded advisors Play an active role in the TBI global Health community beyond Africa, contributing to global strategy and sharing knowledge generated from our work in Africa with other regions. Offer and Solution Development Work with Africa leadership, Global Experts health team and country teams to continuously strengthen and evolve TBI’s health offer, including addressing challenges related to cost, quality of care, delivery methodology, and technology adoption. Lead the design and development of programmes to deliver high-impact projects, complementing hands-on advisory support Identify emerging trends and opportunities to inform new solutions, drawing on TBI’s global community of practice Collaborate with Global team colleagues across functional and sectoral areas to develop integrated solutions to complex, cross-cutting challenges Leading TBI Africa’s Health Community of Practice Capture and codify best practices across the portfolio, creating agile feedback loops to strengthen solutions and delivery (in collaboration with performance and impact teams) Lead the Health Community of Practice in TBI Africa, contributing to institutional knowledge and cross-sector learning Collaborate with the global health practitioners group and Global Experts health team to ensure effective knowledge sharing across the organisation Support robust data stewardship of health programmes across country and regional teams Deploy expertise, understanding and networks in African regional and global health organisations to support positioning of our country work. Internal Leadership Provide leadership and, where applicable, line management to advisors, associates, and junior staff Lead programme development and oversee implementation. Foster a positive, high-performance culture that drives innovation and delivery Contribute to broader Institute priorities by providing practical insights and recommendations to strengthen TBI’s global health work and policy initiatives Business Development and Relationship Management Work closely with regional and global account management teams to develop impactful health programmes aligned with political leaders’ priorities at country and regional levels Lead business development efforts and implementation of TBI’s fundraising strategy in collaboration with leadership and fundraising teams. Build and deploy personal networks to proactively engage with potential funders, identifying priorities and funding opportunities. Working with others, secure funding for TBI’s health work in Africa, enabling us to expand our support to governments . Manage relationships with technical leads and focal points in partner organisations involved in co-implementation, as well as in relevant regional health organisations. Maintain relationships with technical counterparts and investment leads from institutional donors, as required. External Engagement and Partnerships Represent TBI as a senior subject-matter expert at high-profile external events, including delivering presentations Work with Africa leadership to strengthen networks and partnerships with leading health innovators and stakeholders Engage with donors, non-profit organisations, media, suppliers, and other external partners to support TBI’s strategic and financial objectives Person Specification The successful candidate will demonstrate: Extensive practical experience in the health sector in Africa, advising political leaders on complex public health and innovation challenges, or experience in government or leading advisory organisations Credibility across a wide range of health topics, with deep expertise in one or more areas. Strong understanding of emerging innovations and technologies in health, with the ability to support leaders in scaling these solutions A well-developed problem-solving toolkit, with the ability to design creative solutions to complex challenges and lead large programmes of work to a successful conclusion. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence diverse audiences Strong attention to detail and a proven ability to deliver high-quality outputs under tight deadlines Recognised thought leadership, with the ability to represent the Institute through publications and high-level engagements Effective leadership skills, with a willingness to be hands-on to achieve results Ability to communicate and influence at all levels, from senior executives to technical teams. Comfort working in ambiguous, fast-paced, and matrixed environments, with the ability to drive impact at scale A track record of building and sustaining strategic partnerships across public, private, and non-profit sectors, preferably including fundraising Experience working across multiple countries in Africa, and in diverse resource settings is desirable Additional Requirements Fluency in English is required; proficiency in an additional language is an advantage Willingness to travel within Africa (approximately 30%) Working Arrangement This is a fixed-term role with an initial contract duration of two years. The successful candidate may be based in any African country where the Tony Blair Institute has an office, subject to the Institute's employment policies and the candidate's right to work. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. We encourage early applications, as we reserve the right to close the vacancy once a suitable candidate has been identified. Closing Date: 2026-08-04
item.dc:creator: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change

item.title: Director of Applications
item.description: We don’t just talk, we do. Lead the change with us. At the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, we work with political leaders around the world to drive change. We help governments turn bold ideas into reality so they can deliver for their people. We do it by advising on strategy, policy and delivery, unlocking the power of technology across all three. And by sharing what we learn on the ground, so everyone can benefit. We do it to build more open, inclusive and prosperous countries for people everywhere. We are a global team of over 800 changemakers, operating in more than 40 countries, across five continents. We are political strategists, policy experts, delivery practitioners, technology specialists and more. We speak more than 45 languages. We are working on over 100 projects, tackling some of the world’s biggest challenges. We’re all here at TBI to make a difference. In a world of ever more complex challenges, we believe diversity of background and perspective is a strength. We pride ourselves on a culture that values and nurtures difference. We are dedicated to unlocking potential, not only for the countries we work in but also for each of our team members. No matter where you’re from or who you are, if you’re passionate about the transformative power of progressive politics, we invite you to build a better future with us. Role Summary TBI's Digital function exists to enable the Institute's global mission by ensuring our people have the platforms, applications and engineering capability to operate effectively at scale. Following a period of foundational delivery, including the establishment of Cyber Essentials+ certification, a live SOC, Salesforce as our CRM platform, the stabilisation of our Workday investment, and the building of our data warehouse foundations, Digital is moving into a phase focused on platform maturity, AI-enabled operations and engineering excellence. The Director of Applications is a new, senior role accountable for the availability, configuration and ongoing evolution of TBI's core business platforms: Salesforce, Workday, and Microsoft 365 / Azure, together with the engineering practice, AI platform operations and platform-level roadmaps that underpin them. Reporting to the CTO, this role leads the Applications function, owns the platform Centre of Excellence, and provides the technical leadership that translates business needs into delivered platform capability. You will be an experienced applications leader with deep expertise in SaaS platforms, modern engineering practice (CI/CD, source control, environment and release management, test automation) and the operational realities of running mission-critical business systems globally. You will work closely with the CTO, the Head of Digital Transformation & Performance, the Enterprise Architect, the Director of Security & Compliance, and the platform leads to ensure our applications estate enables rather than constrains TBI's ambitions. Key Responsibilities Platform Leadership Accountable for the availability, configuration and ongoing management of TBI's three strategic platforms: Salesforce, Workday, and Microsoft 365 / Azure Own the platform-level roadmaps that enable delivery of the Digital Roadmap, in close collaboration with the Digital Leadership Team and Platform Leads Establish and lead the platform Centre of Excellence; platform-specific standards, delivery patterns and an internal community across the platform leads and developers Line management responsibility for the Build team Engineering Practice Own engineering practice across the Applications function: source control and repository governance (GitHub, Salesforce via Gearset), CI/CD pipelines, environment management (dev/test/UAT/prod parity and access), release management and test strategy / automation Define and enforce technical execution standards for releases, distinct from the change control approval process Define and lead engineering standards, architectural patterns and Infrastructure as Code AI Platform Operations Accountable for the operational availability of TBI's internal AI platforms (Claude, Agentforce, AI assistants) and ongoing AI consumption cost and budget management Own AI usage monitoring: drift, hallucination and misuse pattern detection, partnering with the Director of Security & Compliance, who owns AI security and the Responsible Use Policy Provide the operational foundation that enables TBI's Agentic AI and Generative AI capability pillars Capacity & Observability Own scaling (design and operation), rightsizing and optimisation of cloud and SaaS resources Own application monitoring, alerting and on-call response for in-scope platforms Lead cross-platform test strategy and quality standards Cross-Functional Working Partner with the Digital Leadership to translate business needs (the Digital Roadmap) into platform-level delivery (Platform Roadmaps) Partner with the Director of Security & Compliance on identity, access, secret management and DevSecOps practices embedded in CI/CD Partner with the Enterprise Architect on significant technical decisions and integration patterns Support the CTO with platform investment cases, vendor negotiation for strategic platforms and Executive-level reporting on platform health Person Specification Significant senior leadership experience running enterprise application functions in a mid-to-large global organisation Deep expertise in at least two of: Salesforce, Workday, and Microsoft 365 / Azure or AWS as enterprise platforms (configuration, integration, governance, licensing, support) Demonstrable experience embedding modern engineering practice incl. CI/CD pipelines, source control governance, environment management, release management, automated testing, in a SaaS-heavy environment Track record of building and running Centres of Excellence around major platforms Experience operationalising AI platforms and managing AI consumption cost is a strong advantage Strong understanding of how applications-layer decisions intersect with security, identity, data and architecture, and a collaborative working style with peers in those functions Experience managing technical leads and platform specialists, and a track record of developing them Comfortable operating at executive level on platform investment cases and roadmap trade-offs Pragmatic and delivery-oriented; able to make and defend decisions about where to invest engineering effort Open and progressive; bold and pragmatic; an optimistic changemaker — aligned with TBI's core beliefs Candidates must have the legal right to work in the UK to apply for this role. We reserve the right to close this role early if we receive a sufficient number of applications. Closing Date: 2026-08-09
item.dc:creator: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change

item.title: Director of Security & Compliance
item.description: We don’t just talk, we do. Lead the change with us. At the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, we work with political leaders around the world to drive change. We help governments turn bold ideas into reality so they can deliver for their people. We do it by advising on strategy, policy and delivery, unlocking the power of technology across all three. And by sharing what we learn on the ground, so everyone can benefit. We do it to build more open, inclusive and prosperous countries for people everywhere. We are a global team of over 800 changemakers, operating in more than 40 countries, across five continents. We are political strategists, policy experts, delivery practitioners, technology specialists and more. We speak more than 45 languages. We are working on over 100 projects, tackling some of the world’s biggest challenges. We’re all here at TBI to make a difference. In a world of ever more complex challenges, we believe diversity of background and perspective is a strength. We pride ourselves on a culture that values and nurtures difference. We are dedicated to unlocking potential, not only for the countries we work in but also for each of our team members. No matter where you’re from or who you are, if you’re passionate about the transformative power of progressive politics, we invite you to build a better future with us. Role Summary TBI operates globally in politically sensitive environments, handling sensitive policy, advisory and donor information across multiple jurisdictions. Following the establishment of Cyber Essentials+ certification, a live outsourced SOC and a 24/7 monitoring posture, Digital is now entering a phase of security and compliance maturity that includes ISO 27001 ambition, AI security and governance across a rapidly expanding AI estate, and a deepened compliance posture across the regulatory regimes in which TBI operates. The Director of Security & Compliance is a new, senior role accountable for the security, governance, risk, compliance, continuity and data protection posture of TBI. Reporting to the CTO, this role leads the Security & Compliance function - the IT Security Engineer and the outsourced SOC - and provides authoritative leadership on cyber, AI and information security across the Institute. It is the named accountable owner for our certifications, our risk register, our continuity arrangements and the security and governance of our AI estate. You will be an experienced security and compliance leader with deep expertise in modern cyber operations, governance frameworks (ISO 27001 in particular), risk management and the security and governance of AI in an enterprise context. You will work closely with Legal & Risk, the Director of Applications, the Enterprise Architect and the CTO to ensure our security posture enables rather than constrains TBI's mission. Key Responsibilities Security Operations Accountable for security monitoring, incident response, remediation and security tooling / platform management across TBI (both internal and external) Own the relationship with the outsourced SOC and line manage the IT Security Engineer Own the firm-wide security awareness programme - phishing simulation, user training and security culture (delivered through a vCISO arrangement where appropriate) Own the design of identity, access (RBAC) and authentication (MFA / SSO) standards, partnering with the Global Service Desk Manager who owns day-to-day operation Governance, Risk & Compliance Own TBI's IT governance framework adoption and maintenance, including the supporting RAPID and accountability mapping Own the regulatory and standards compliance posture - GDPR, ISO 27001, Cyber Essentials+, and others - including certification maintenance, control evidence, audit readiness and statutory reporting Lead TBI's path to ISO 27001 certification as a strategic programme, including the definition of certification scope across emerging functions and capabilities Own the central Digital & Data Security risk register - identification, assessment, mitigation and ongoing maintenance - in partnership with Legal & Risk Data Protection & Privacy Own the data classification framework (design and operation), data retention policies and the GDPR / data subject rights operating model Partner with Legal on the firm-wide data protection programme and breach notification process Continuity Own disaster recovery and business continuity planning, testing and recovery - including RTO / RPO definition for critical systems and the incident communications plan AI Security & Governance Own the security posture of TBI's AI estate - access and authentication for AI platforms, prompt and output data protection, prompt injection and jailbreak defence, and third-party AI vendor risk assessment (Anthropic, OpenAI, Salesforce Einstein, and others) Own TBI's Responsible Use Policy for AI tools across the firm Partner with the Director of Applications, the Enterprise Architect and the CTO to ensure all AI initiatives across TBI - including AI capability developed outside of Digital - sit within an appropriate security and governance envelope DevSecOps Own DevSecOps practice: secret management, vulnerability scanning (SAST / DAST / dependency scanning), pipeline security gates, container and workload security and code-level vulnerability management Partner with the Director of Applications to embed security controls into the engineering practice without becoming a delivery blocker Third-Party Risk Own third-party security risk assessment and ongoing vendor security monitoring, in partnership with the Head of Digital Transformation & Performance, who owns the vendor relationship and performance lifecycle Person Specification Significant senior leadership experience in security and compliance roles within a mid-to-large global organisation Deep, demonstrable expertise in ISO 27001 - ideally as named lead on a successful certification programme - together with working knowledge of SOC 2, NIST CSF and other relevant frameworks Strong technical credibility across modern security operations: SIEM, EDR, IAM, vulnerability management and DevSecOps tooling Demonstrable expertise in GDPR and data protection in a global, multi-jurisdiction context Demonstrable understanding of AI security and AI governance - prompt injection, model risk, third-party AI vendor risk and the operational realities of running an AI estate at scale Experience designing and running risk registers and risk processes at organisational level Strong stakeholder credibility at executive level; able to articulate security posture in business terms and influence non-technical audiences Pragmatic, proportionate and able to balance security rigour with delivery velocity Experience managing technical security staff and outsourced security partners Comfortable operating in politically sensitive contexts and exercising judgement on disclosure, escalation and risk acceptance Qualifications & Certifications Essential One or more of these certifications CISSP (ISC) CISM (ISACA) ISO 27001 Lead Implementer CIPP/E or CIPM (IAPP) CRISC or CGEIT (ISACA) CCSP AIGP Candidates must have the legal right to work in the UK to apply for this role. We reserve the right to close this role early if we receive a sufficient number of applications. Closing Date: 2026-08-09
item.dc:creator: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change

item.title: Service Desk Analyst
item.description: We don’t just talk, we do. Lead the change with us. At the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, we work with political leaders around the world to drive change. We help governments turn bold ideas into reality so they can deliver for their people. We do it by advising on strategy, policy and delivery, unlocking the power of technology across all three. And by sharing what we learn on the ground, so everyone can benefit. We do it to build more open, inclusive and prosperous countries for people everywhere. We are a global team of over 800 changemakers, operating in more than 40 countries, across five continents. We are political strategists, policy experts, delivery practitioners, technology specialists and more. We speak more than 45 languages. We are working on over 100 projects, tackling some of the world’s biggest challenges. We’re all here at TBI to make a difference. In a world of ever more complex challenges, we believe diversity of background and perspective is a strength. We pride ourselves on a culture that values and nurtures difference. We are dedicated to unlocking potential, not only for the countries we work in but also for each of our team members. No matter where you’re from or who you are, if you’re passionate about the transformative power of progressive politics, we invite you to build a better future with us. Role Summary We are seeking a highly motivated and customer-focused individual to join our IT Service Desk team based in our London HQ. The successful candidate will be the first point of contact for all IT-related issues and requests, providing support to end-users. This role requires excellent communication skills, a strong technical understanding, and a passion for delivering exceptional customer service. Key Responsibilities Global User Support: Provide responsive 1st and 2nd line technical support to TBI’s end users, enabling them to undertake their roles in a secure and professional environment. Working collaboratively with our helpdesks in Nairobi, Dubai and Singapore to handover tickets and tasks, ensuring that our staff receive timely assistance wherever they are located globally. Incident Management: Respond to and resolve IT service requests and incidents via phone, email, chat or in-person, ensuring timely resolution and maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction. Troubleshooting: Diagnose and troubleshoot hardware and software, escalating more complex issues to senior analysts. Onboarding: Onboarding new members of staff, ensuring that their IT services, laptop and other devices are ready to go on day one. Knowledge Base: Contribute to the development and maintenance of a knowledge base, creating and updating support documentation and user guides. Asset Management: Workingto ensure all end user IT assets are captured and the asset management system is kept up to date. Security: Working with the Cyber Security team to assist in maintaining the security compliance across the end user environment (laptop, mobile devices) including multi factor authentication, access to data, anti-virus protection, threat detection and loss of service. Compliance: Ensure all IT activities comply with company policies, procedures, and security standards. Person Specification Essential Previous experience working within an IT Support Role ITIL foundation qualification Excellent communication skills, including the ability to communicate technical information to non-technical stakeholders. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills to identify and resolve technical issues. Capable of working individually and as part of a team. Knowledge & experience of working with Window 10/11 macOS/iOS Microsoft 365 Entra (Azure Active Directory) Exchange Online MDM Tools ITSM application or other IT service desk ticket management tools. Demonstrates the ability to prioritise against tight deadlines. Ability to take the lead on projects and problems when requested or as appropriate. Self-starter, able to take initiative and find a resolution to new issues and share knowledge with other team members. Desirable Experience providing support to senior executives/VIPs. Experience of working within a head office environment. Experience of working in a global service desk function. Knowledge & experience of working with: Slack Freshservice Video Conferencing Closing Date: 2026-08-22
item.dc:creator: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change

item.title: Talent Pool Contingent Upon Funding - Financing & Fundraising Expert– Energy Access Programme
item.description: We don’t just talk, we do. Lead the change with us. At the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, we work with political leaders around the world to drive change. We help governments turn bold ideas into reality so they can deliver for their people. We do it by advising on strategy, policy and delivery, unlocking the power of technology across all three. And by sharing what we learn on the ground, so everyone can benefit. We do it to build more open, inclusive and prosperous countries for people everywhere. We are a global team of over 800 changemakers, operating in more than 40 countries, across five continents. We are political strategists, policy experts, delivery practitioners, technology specialists and more. We speak more than 45 languages. We are working on over 100 projects, tackling some of the world’s biggest challenges. We’re all here at TBI to make a difference. In a world of ever more complex challenges, we believe diversity of background and perspective is a strength. We pride ourselves on a culture that values and nurtures difference. We are dedicated to unlocking potential, not only for the countries we work in but also for each of our team members. No matter where you’re from or who you are, if you’re passionate about the transformative power of progressive politics, we invite you to build a better future with us. Role Summary Our client is seeking a Financing & Fundraising Expert to support resource mobilisation, investment planning, and financing strategies for energy sector initiatives. The role will focus on identifying funding opportunities, supporting investment readiness, and strengthening financing approaches that accelerate programme delivery and long-term sustainability. Role and Responsibilities Support development of financing and resource mobilisation strategies. Contribute to investment planning and project financing approaches. Support engagement with financiers, development partners, investors, and funding institutions. Conduct financial analysis and provide recommendations to support decision-making. Contribute to development of bankable opportunities and investment pipelines. Support innovative financing approaches and partnership development. Contribute to production of financial reports, analyses, and stakeholder-facing materials. Key Deliverables Financing Strategy and Financing Matrix. Fundraising and Resource Mobilisation Strategy. Bankable Project Pipeline and Funding Analysis Reports. Financing Opportunities Assessment across Energy and Related Sectors. Quarterly Financial Progress and Resource Mobilisation Reports. Requirements Experience Required Minimum 8 years of experience in project finance, investment banking, infrastructure finance, or DFI-funded programmes. Proven experience mobilising investment and donor financing. Technical Requirements Master’s Degree in Finance , Economics, Business Administration, or related field. Expertise in PPPs, blended finance, and project finance. Strong financial modelling and analytical capability. Other Skills Negotiation and relationship management. Strategic thinking. Strong communication and presentation skills. Working Arrangement Based full-time in Lilongwe, with periodic travel within Malawi as required . Terms of Agreement Start Date: To be confirmed Contracting Period: 12 months Important Notice This exercise is intended to identify and establish a pool of qualified candidates for this potential future opportun ity . Participation does not constitute an offer of employment or guarantee appointment to any position. Closing Date: 2026-07-31
item.dc:creator: Tony Blair Institute for Global Change

item.title: Talent Pool Contingent Upon Funding - Team Leader - Energy Access Programme
item.description: We don’t just talk, we do. Lead the change with us. At the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change, we work with political leaders around the world to drive change. We help governments turn bold ideas into reality so they can deliver for their people. We do it by advising on strategy, policy and delivery, unlocking the power of technology across all three. And by sharing what we learn on the ground, so everyone can benefit. We do it to build more open, inclusive and prosperous countries for people everywhere. We are a global team of over 800 changemakers, operating in more than 40 countries, across five continents. We are political strategists, policy experts, delivery practitioners, technology specialists and more. We speak more than 45 languages. We are working on over 100 projects, tackling some of the world’s biggest challenges. We’re all here at TBI to make a difference. In a world of ever more complex challenges, we believe diversity of background and perspective is a strength. We pride ourselves on a culture that values and nurtures difference. We are dedicated to unlocking potential, not only for the countries we work in but also for each of our team members. No matter where you’re from or who you are, if you’re passionate about the transformative power of progressive politics, we invite you to build a better future with us. Role Summary Our client is seeking an experienced Team Leader to provide strategic leadership and oversight for a large-scale energy sector programme. The role will focus on driving programme delivery, coordinating diverse stakeholders, strengthening institutional performance, and ensuring the successful execution of strategic priorities. This position offers an opportunity to contribute to transformational development outcomes through leadership, delivery management, and stakeholder engagement. Role and Responsibilities Provide strategic leadership and oversight across programme workstreams. Drive implementation of programme priorities and delivery objectives. Coordinate stakeholders across government, development partners, the private sector, and implementing institutions. Establish effective governance, reporting, and performance management mechanisms. Lead programme planning, prioritisation, and resource coordination. Promote institutional strengthening and sustainable delivery practices. Produce strategic updates and recommendations for senior decision-makers. Requirements Experience Required Minimum 10 years of progressively responsible experience in programme leadership, infrastructure, energy, public sector delivery, or related fields. Demonstrated experience leading large-scale, multi-stakeholder initiatives. Experience working with government institutions, development partners, and senior decision-makers. Technical Requirements Master’s Degree in Energy, Engineering, Economics, Development Studies, Project Management, Business Administration, or related field. Strong programme leadership and delivery management expertise. Knowledge of energy sector planning, financing, and implementation. Strong analytical and reporting capabilities. Other Skills Strong leadership and decision-making capability. Excellent stakeholder management and negotiation skills. Strategic thinking and problem-solving ability.
